Class GameState

java.lang.Object
sims.gameEngine.GameState
public class GameState extends Object
Represents the overall state of the game simulation.

The GameState class acts as the central container for all active game entities, including Sims, homes, careers, and locations. It provides a snapshot of the current simulation, allowing the game engine to track, update, and persist progress across sessions.

Responsibilities:

  • Maintain a list of all active Sim objects in the game.
  • Track homes, locations, and activities associated with Sims.
  • Store global simulation data such as time, resources, and upgrades.
  • Provide methods for querying and updating the current state.
  • Serve as the entry point for saving and loading game progress.

Usage Example:

GameState gameState = new GameState();
List sims = SimFactory.defaultGame();
gameState.setSimList(sims);

// Query current Sims
for (Sim sim : gameState.getSimList()) {
    System.out.println(sim.getName() + " is part of the game.");
}

By centralizing all simulation data, GameState ensures consistency across gameplay mechanics and provides a single source of truth for the current game world.

  • Constructor Details

    • GameState

      public GameState()

  • Method Details

    • startGame

      public void startGame()
      Initializes the simulation by adding a default Sim and several OutsideLocation instances with associated activities. This setup defines the environment in which Sims can interact, including leisure, social, and work-related activities.

      The following locations are created:

      • Gym – contains a "Workout" activity.
      • Park – contains "Play ball games" and "Talk to friends" activities.
      • Mall – contains "Eat at Restaurant", "Arcade", and "Watch Movie" activities.
      • IT Office, Bank, Hospital – each contains a "Work" activity.

      Each activity specifies its duration, the need it fulfills (e.g., Fun, Social, Hunger, Salary), and the benefit values associated with completing it.

      This method demonstrates how to populate the repository of locations and activities so that crawler Sims can explore and engage with different environments.

    • getGameState

      public int getGameState()
      Retrieves the current state of the game.

      The game state is represented as an integer, which can be used to indicate different phases or modes of the game

      Returns:
      the current game state as an integer

    • addSim

      public void addSim(Sim sim)
      Adds a new Sim to the game.

      This method appends the provided Sim object to the list of Sims currently managed by the game. It allows new characters to be introduced into the simulation.

      Parameters:
      sim - the Sim object to be added to the game

    • addLocation

      public void addLocation(OutsideLocation location)
      Adds a new outside location to the game.

      This method appends the provided OutsideLocation object to the list of available outside locations. It allows the game world to expand by introducing new areas that Sims can interact with.

      Parameters:
      location - the OutsideLocation object to be added to the game

    • getGameRunning

      public boolean getGameRunning()
      Checks whether the game is currently running.

      This method returns the value of the gameRunning flag, which indicates if the simulation is active. It can be used to determine whether the game loop should continue executing or if the game has been stopped/paused.

      Returns:
      true if the game is running, false otherwise

    • setGameState

      public void setGameState(int state)
      Updates the current state of the game.

      This method sets the gameState field to the specified value. The state can be used to represent different phases or modes of the game

      Parameters:
      state - the new game state to be applied

    • update

      public void update()
      Updates the game flow based on the current state.

      This method uses the gameState value to determine which menu or action should be displayed or executed. Each state corresponds to a different phase of the game:

      • 0 – Show the main menu
      • 1 – Show the choose Sim menu
      • 2 – Show the create Sim menu
      • 3 – Show the action menu
      • 4 – Show the move menu
      • 5 – Reserved/unused state
      • 6 – End the game

      Implementing this switch-case structure ensures that the game responds appropriately to user input and transitions smoothly between different stages.

    • showMainMenu

      public void showMainMenu()
      Displays the main menu of the game and handles user input.

      This method prints the main menu options to the console, allowing the player to select actions such as choosing a character, creating a new character, saving the game, or ending the game. Based on the player's choice, the gameState is updated accordingly:

      • 1 – Select Character (transition to choose Sim menu)
      • 2 – Create Character (transition to create Sim menu)
      • 3 – Save Game (transition to end game state)
      • 4 – End Game (not yet implemented in this snippet)

      The method uses readInt to validate user input and ensure the choice falls within the available options.

    • showChooseSimMenu

      public void showChooseSimMenu()
      Displays the character selection menu and allows the player to choose a Sim.

      This method prints a list of all available Sims from simList, prompting the player to select one. The chosen Sim becomes the currentSim, and the gameState is updated to 3 (action menu). After updating the state, the update() method is called to continue the game flow.

      Menu flow:

      • Displays all Sims with their names and corresponding index numbers.
      • Prompts the player for input using readInt, ensuring the choice is within the valid range.
      • Sets currentSim to the selected Sim.
      • Transitions the game to the action menu (state 3).

    • showCreateMenu

      public void showCreateMenu()
      Displays the Sim creation menu and allows the player to create a new Sim.

      This method guides the player through the process of creating a Sim by:

      • Prompting for the Sim's name and age.
      • Allowing the player to choose whether the Sim has a job.
      • If a job is selected, prompting for a job sector (IT, Finance, Healthcare).
      • Assigning the chosen career sector to the new Career object.

      The method uses readString and readInt to validate user input and ensure correct values are provided. Once complete, the new Sim can be added to the game world with the specified attributes.

    • showActionMenu

      public void showActionMenu()
      Displays the action menu for the currently selected Sim and handles player input.

      This method provides the player with a variety of options depending on the Sim's current location, available activities, upgrades, and relationships. It dynamically builds a menu of actions and executes the chosen option.

      Features:

      • Displays the Sim's current location.
      • Shows the current time and Sim statistics.
      • Lists other Sims present in the same location.
      • Builds a list of available activities from the location and unlocked upgrades.
      • Allows the player to purchase home upgrades to unlock new activities.
      • Provides interaction options with other Sims (talk, add friend).
      • Provides interaction options with existing friends (message, visit).
      • Allows the player to move to a different location.
      • Allows the player to exit back to the main menu.

      Game State Transitions:

      • Move Location → gameState = 4
      • Exit to Main Menu → gameState = 0
      • Other actions may trigger activities, relationship changes, or upgrades.

      The method uses a Map of menu options to Runnable actions, ensuring that each choice executes the correct behavior. Input validation is handled via readInt, and insufficient funds are checked before performing activities or purchasing upgrades.

    • showMoveMenu

      public void showMoveMenu()
      Displays the location movement menu and allows the player to move the current Sim.

      This method builds a list of possible destinations based on the Sim's current location and career requirements:

      • If the Sim is in a HomeLocation, all other rooms in the home are listed.
        • invalid input: '<'liIf the Sim is outside, their home is added as a destination.
      • OutsideLocations are added if they either have no requirement or match the Sim's career sector.

      The menu is printed with numbered options, and the player selects a destination using readInt. The chosen location’s moveTo method is then called to move the Sim. After moving, the gameState is set to 3 (action menu), and update() is invoked to continue the game flow.

      Game State Transition:

      • After moving → gameState = 3 (Action Menu)

    • readInt

      public int readInt(String prompt)
      Reads an integer value from user input with validation.

      This method displays the given prompt and waits for the user to enter an integer. Input is validated to ensure that:

      • The entered value is an integer (non-numeric input is rejected).
      • The value is greater than zero (non-positive values are rejected).

      If invalid input is detected, the user is prompted again until a valid integer is entered. The method uses a Scanner instance to read input from the console.

      Parameters:
      prompt - the message displayed to the user before input
      Returns:
      a positive integer entered by the user

    • readInt

      public int readInt(String prompt, int options)
      Reads an integer value from user input with validation against a range of options.

      This method displays the given prompt and waits for the user to enter an integer. Input is validated to ensure that:

      • The entered value is an integer (non-numeric input is rejected).
      • The value is greater than zero.
      • The value does not exceed the specified number of options.

      If invalid input is detected, the user is prompted again until a valid integer is entered. The method uses a Scanner instance to read input from the console.

      Parameters:
      prompt - the message displayed to the user before input
      options - the maximum valid option number (upper bound of the range)
      Returns:
      a positive integer between 1 and options, inclusive

    • readString

      public String readString(String prompt)
      Reads a string value from user input.

      This method displays the given prompt and waits for the user to enter a line of text. The input is read using a Scanner instance and returned as a String.

      Parameters:
      prompt - the message displayed to the user before input
      Returns:
      the string entered by the user

    • showStats

      public void showStats(Sim sim)
      Displays the current statistics of the specified Sim.

      This method prints the Sim's name followed by each of their needs and the corresponding values. The values are formatted to two decimal places and shown as a fraction of 100, representing the Sim's status in different areas (e.g., hunger, energy, social).

      Output Example:

      Alice's Stats
Social : 75.50/100
Hunger : 60.25/100
Energy : 90.00/100
Bladder : 80.00/100
Hygiene : 50.00/100
Fun : 95.00/100
      Parameters:
      sim - the Sim whose statistics are to be displayed

    • showTime

      public void showTime()
      Displays the current in-game time.

      This method formats the hours and minutes fields into a standard 24-hour clock format (HH:mm) and prints it to the console. It also prints the integer representation of the time by calling getIntTime(), which can be useful for calculations or comparisons.

      Output Example:

      Current Time: 09:05
Int time : 905

    • decayLoop

      public void decayLoop(int duration, String need)
      Runs the decay loop for all Sims over a specified duration.

      This method simulates the passage of time in the game by repeatedly updating the in-game clock and applying need decay to each Sim. The decay is applied to the specified need type for all Sims in simList, using the current Sim and the integer time value as context.

      Behavior:

      • For each unit of duration, the in-game time is updated.
      • Each Sim in simList has performDecay called for the given need.
      • The getIntTime() method is used to provide the current time in integer format to the decay logic.
      Parameters:
      duration - the number of time units to simulate
      need - the type of need to decay (e.g., Hunger, Energy, Social)

    • getIntTime

      public int getIntTime()
      Returns the current in-game time as an integer value.

      The time is calculated by converting the hours and minutes fields into total minutes since midnight: 

      intTime = (hours * 60) + minutes

      This integer representation of time is useful for calculations, comparisons, and passing into methods that require a numeric time format (e.g., decay loops or activity scheduling).

      Returns:
      the current time in minutes since midnight

    • getStringTime

      public String getStringTime(int minutes)
      Converts a time value in minutes into a formatted string.

      This method takes a total number of minutes and converts it into hours and days where applicable:

      • If the value is less than 60, it is displayed as minutes only.
      • If the value is 60 or more, it is converted into hours and minutes.
      • If the value exceeds 24 hours, it is converted into days, hours, and minutes.

      Output Examples:

      getStringTime(45)   → "45 Minutes"
getStringTime(125)  → "02 Hours : 05 Minutes"
getStringTime(1500) → "01 Day : 01 Hours : 00 Minutes"
      Parameters:
      minutes - the total time in minutes
      Returns:
      a formatted string representing the time in days, hours, and minutes

    • updateTime

      public void updateTime()
      Updates the in-game clock by advancing time.

      This method increments the minutes field and carries over values into higher units of time as needed:

      • Minutes → Hours (every 60 minutes)
      • Hours → Days (every 24 hours)
      • Days → Months (every 31 days, simplified assumption)
      • Months → Years (every 12 months)

      The rollover logic ensures that the in-game time remains consistent and progresses naturally. For simplicity, months are assumed to have 31 days.

      Example:

      Initial: 23 hours, 59 minutes
After updateTime():
  → 0 hours, 0 minutes, +1 day

    • endGame

      public void endGame()
      Ends the game by stopping the main loop.

      This method sets the gameRunning flag to false, which signals the game engine to terminate execution. Once called, the game will no longer continue updating or processing menus.

      Usage:

      • Typically invoked when the player selects "End Game" from the main menu.
      • Ensures a clean exit from the game loop without abrupt termination.
